proven.claim: PROVEN: verdict is always in {proven=0, violation=1, error=2}
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 44
; PROVEN: verdict is always in {proven=0, violation=1, error=2}
; The nested ternary at lines 47-50 covers every (expected, result) pair.

(declare-const expected Int)  ; 0=unsat, 1=sat
(declare-const result  Int)   ; 0=unsat, 1=sat, 2=unknown, 3=error
(declare-const verdict Int)   ; 0=proven, 1=violation, 2=error

; Domain constraints
(assert (or (= expected 0) (= expected 1)))
(assert (>= result 0))
(assert (<= result 3))

; Code transition: expected=0 (unsat) branch
(assert (=> (and (= expected 0) (= result 0))
            (= verdict 0)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 0) (= result 1))
            (= verdict 1)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 0) (and (not (= result 0)) (not (= result 1))))
            (= verdict 2)))

; Code transition: expected=1 (sat) branch
(assert (=> (and (= expected 1) (= result 1))
            (= verdict 1)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 1) (= result 0))
            (= verdict 0)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 1) (and (not (= result 0)) (not (= result 1))))
            (= verdict 2)))

; Negation: verdict escapes the valid range
(assert (or (< verdict 0) (> verdict 2)))
(check-sat)

proven.claim: PROVEN: A definitive Z3 result (sat or unsat) never yields verdict=error.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 44
; PROVEN: A definitive Z3 result (sat or unsat) never yields verdict=error.
; verdict=error is only reachable when result=unknown or result=error (2,3).

(declare-const expected Int)  ; 0=unsat, 1=sat
(declare-const result  Int)   ; 0=unsat (0) or 1=sat ONLY
(declare-const verdict Int)

; Domain: expected is binary; result is definitive
(assert (or (= expected 0) (= expected 1)))
(assert (or (= result 0) (= result 1)))

; Code transitions: both branches of the ternary, definitive results only
(assert (=> (and (= expected 0) (= result 0)) (= verdict 0)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 0) (= result 1)) (= verdict 1)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 1) (= result 1)) (= verdict 1)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 1) (= result 0)) (= verdict 0)))

; Negation: verdict = error
(assert (= verdict 2))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: Vacuity bypass: applyAxioms calls verifyBlock(check.smt2) directly (line 45),
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 44
; Vacuity bypass: applyAxioms calls verifyBlock(check.smt2) directly (line 45),
; bypassing the isVacuous filter in verifyAll. A vacuous formula — one with
; declared vars but no assertions relating them — is trivially satisfiable.
; For expected="unsat" (all P1 and P2 checks), sat → verdict="violation",
; producing a false positive with no real code transition modelled.

(declare-const has_real_transitions Int) ; 1 = formula has ≥1 assert referencing 2+ vars
(declare-const z3_result Int)            ; 0=unsat, 1=sat
(declare-const expected Int)             ; 0=unsat (P1/P2 checks always use expected=unsat)
(declare-const verdict Int)              ; 0=proven, 1=violation, 2=error

; Code transition: vacuous formula — vars declared but no cross-var constraint
(assert (= has_real_transitions 0))

; Code transition: vacuous formula is trivially satisfiable; Z3 returns sat
(assert (=> (= has_real_transitions 0) (= z3_result 1)))

; Code transition: applyP1 and applyP2 always set expected="unsat" (they prove properties)
(assert (= expected 0))

; Code transition: verdict logic — expected=unsat, result=sat → violation
(assert (=> (and (= expected 0) (= z3_result 1)) (= verdict 1)))

; Reachability: vacuous formula produces false "violation" verdict
(assert (= has_real_transitions 0))
(assert (= verdict 1))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: Semantic: applyP1 constructs its check formula as:
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 44
; Semantic: applyP1 constructs its check formula as:
;   comment_header + proven.smt2
; proven.smt2 was already verified unsat (it IS the proven property).
; Re-running the identical formula always returns unsat → verdict="proven"
; for EVERY caller, regardless of whether that caller establishes the precondition.
; The caller's own context (its proven assertions, its constraints) is never injected.

(declare-const caller_assertions_in_formula Int) ; count of caller-specific asserts added
(declare-const proven_smt2_was_unsat Int)        ; 1 = already verified unsat
(declare-const z3_result Int)                    ; 0=unsat, 1=sat
(declare-const verdict Int)                      ; 0=proven, 1=violation

; Code transition: applyP1 concatenates only a comment + proven.smt2 (no caller asserts)
(assert (= caller_assertions_in_formula 0))

; Code transition: proven.smt2 is in contract.proven → was previously unsat
(assert (= proven_smt2_was_unsat 1))

; Code transition: re-running the same formula returns the same result
(assert (=> (and (= proven_smt2_was_unsat 1) (= caller_assertions_in_formula 0))
            (= z3_result 0)))

; Code transition: expected=unsat, result=unsat → verdict=proven
(assert (=> (= z3_result 0) (= verdict 0)))

; Semantic violation: verdict=proven for every caller regardless of its behavior
; because caller context was never included in the formula
(assert (= caller_assertions_in_formula 0))
(assert (= verdict 0))
(check-sat)

clause_history.clause:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 44
; PROVEN: verdict is always in {proven=0, violation=1, error=2}
; The nested ternary at lines 47-50 covers every (expected, result) pair.

(declare-const expected Int)  ; 0=unsat, 1=sat
(declare-const result  Int)   ; 0=unsat, 1=sat, 2=unknown, 3=error
(declare-const verdict Int)   ; 0=proven, 1=violation, 2=error

; Domain constraints
(assert (or (= expected 0) (= expected 1)))
(assert (>= result 0))
(assert (<= result 3))

; Code transition: expected=0 (unsat) branch
(assert (=> (and (= expected 0) (= result 0))
            (= verdict 0)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 0) (= result 1))
            (= verdict 1)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 0) (and (not (= result 0)) (not (= result 1))))
            (= verdict 2)))

; Code transition: expected=1 (sat) branch
(assert (=> (and (= expected 1) (= result 1))
            (= verdict 1)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 1) (= result 0))
            (= verdict 0)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 1) (and (not (= result 0)) (not (= result 1))))
            (= verdict 2)))

; Negation: verdict escapes the valid range
(assert (or (< verdict 0) (> verdict 2)))
(check-sat)

; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 44

; verdict=error is only reachable when result=unknown or result=error (2,3).

(declare-const expected Int)  ; 0=unsat, 1=sat
(declare-const result  Int)   ; 0=unsat (0) or 1=sat ONLY
(declare-const verdict Int)

; Domain: expected is binary; result is definitive
(assert (or (= expected 0) (= expected 1)))
(assert (or (= result 0) (= result 1)))

; Code transitions: both branches of the ternary, definitive results only
(assert (=> (and (= expected 0) (= result 0)) (= verdict 0)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 0) (= result 1)) (= verdict 1)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 1) (= result 1)) (= verdict 1)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 1) (= result 0)) (= verdict 0)))

; Negation: verdict = error
(assert (= verdict 2))
(check-sat)
